data.title: GAM managers intensify pressure on Yutaka Giken deal, challenging Honda.
data.description: Portfolio managers at GAM are intensifying pressure on the Yutaka Giken deal by confronting the controlling shareholder Honda. The move comes as they seek to push through the agreement and potentially impact the deal's outcome. This escalation in the situation involves significant numbers of invested assets and potential influence on the final decision regarding the deal.

data.title: KPN to commence €250m share buyback alongside €20.0ct dividend per share in 2026, distributing all Free Cash Flow.
data.description: KPN has effectively implemented its strategy, allowing for the continuous return of capital to shareholders. The company unveiled a plan for a €250 million share repurchase, in addition to a projected €20.0ct dividend per share for 2026. This move signifies the distribution of all Free Cash Flow to investors. The Share Buyback initiative is set to commence on January 29, 2026, with a target completion date of June 29, 2026.

data.title: Arthur Hayes predicts US intervention to support Japanese yen could boost Bitcoin; suggests Fed money printing.
data.description: In a recent essay, Arthur Hayes of Maelstrom Fund discussed the potential impact of U.S. authorities intervening to support the Japanese yen. He proposed that a move by the Treasury Department, in collaboration with the Federal Reserve, to sell dollars and purchase yen could address various financial issues for the Trump administration. Hayes argued that this intervention could strengthen the yen against the dollar, benefiting U.S. export competitiveness. Additionally, it may lower Japanese Government Bond yields, reducing the incentive for Japanese investors to switch from U.S. Treasuries to Japan's sovereign debt. Hayes also suggested that expanding the Federal Reserve's balance sheet by printing more money could complement this strategy. This intervention could potentially have positive implications for risk assets like Bitcoin. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitwise's Matt Hougan warns crypto faces scrutiny without US market structure bill, stressing need to become essential.
data.description: Matt Hougan from Bitwise has stated that the cryptocurrency industry may face challenges with regulatory hurdles and lack of trust if the United States does not enact a market structure bill. The absence of such legislation could hinder the growth of the sector and lead to increased skepticism among investors. Hougan's remarks highlight the importance of regulatory clarity in ensuring the future success of cryptocurrencies.

data.title: China approves import of first batch of Nvidia's H200 AI chips, with several hundred thousand chips granted during CEO Jensen Huang's recent trip. Other approvals pending.
data.description: China has authorized the initial importation of Nvidia Corporation's H200 artificial intelligence chips into the country, with reports indicating that the first batch has been approved for three major Chinese internet firms. The exact number of companies receiving approval and the selection criteria used by Beijing remain undisclosed. Nvidia is yet to comment on the matter. The approval encompasses several hundred thousand H200 chips and was granted during Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's recent visit to China, where he launched the company's annual celebrations leading up to the Lunar New Year in mid-February. Huang is expected to engage with prospective partners during his visit as per previous reports.

data.title: Cassava Technologies & AXON Networks partner in Africa for AI adoption & innovation. Cape Town, Jan 28, 2026.
data.description: In Cape Town, South Africa, on January 28, 2026, Cassava Technologies and AXON Networks unveiled a collaborative effort to pioneer Africa's inaugural intelligent network platform. Cassava Technologies, recognized as a prominent African technology provider, has united with AXON Networks, a renowned global leader in network solutions. data.title: Cadeler signs deal to install foundations at large offshore wind farm in Europe in 2028.
data.description: In Denmark, Cadeler has secured a contract to transport and install monopiles and transition pieces for a major offshore wind project in Europe. The agreement involves the deployment of key components at the wind farm site, indicating significant progress in the renewable energy sector.
